memory_seeds.text: I was a research fellow at the Aethon University archive when the Compact arrived. They called it a 'cultural preservation audit.' They burned half the Predecessor collection before we could evacuate it.
The Predecessor structures on Aethon are three times older than anything on Earth. Whoever built them had metallurgy we still can't replicate. I've dedicated my life to understanding why.
I argued with the dean for six hours about the translation of a single glyph. He was wrong. I was right. I still have the notes.

playable.tagline: Scholar. Reads dead languages and live people.
advantages: Five savvy: counters and customs officials soften for him
Notices what a room is about to do before it does
disadvantages: Should not be allowed near the stick, and knows it
Fixes things by describing them accurately
scrawl: You are Bardo. You came out to the Reach to read what the Predecessors left in the rock, and stayed because the rock keeps answering. Mining crews tolerate you; this one feeds you.
Nine days over the drift and your instruments keep catching a resonance nobody hired you to explain. Tove wants everyone in the galley. You bring your notes.
